zh.Ulverston, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾奧爾維斯東
zh.Kells, Carbury, County Kildare, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡布瑞
zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Birmingham於1302年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，他的父親William de Bermingham40歲，母親Matilda Deincourt34歲。他於大約1336年與Lady Elizabeth Multon結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1342年在愛爾蘭被列為chief governor of ireland。

zh-hans.Ulverston, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾奧爾維斯東
zh-hans.Kells, Carbury, County Kildare, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡布瑞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter de Birmingham于1302年出生在英国林可爾史爾时，他的父亲William de Bermingham40岁，母亲Matilda Deincourt34岁。他于大约1336年与Lady Elizabeth Multon结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1342年在爱尔兰被列为chief governor of ireland。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Birmingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Birmingham in the West Midlands. In Domesday Book the name is already found as Bermingeham, but it seems likely that it was originally Beornmundingahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the people of (-inga-) Beornmund’, a personal name composed of the elements beorn ‘young man, warrior’ + mund ‘protection’. This surname is established in Ireland (see Bermingham ).

variants.name: Burningham
meanings: English (Surrey and Hampshire): variant of   Birmingham  .

variants.name: Bermingham
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Sinead.
Irish (of English origin): variant of   Birmingham  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Ireland.

History: The Bermingham family take their surname from lands held at what is now a great city in the West Midlands of England, but it was brought to Ireland as early as the 12th century. The Norman baron Robert de Bermyngeham was a follower of Strongbow (see Clare ); his name is found on a number of Irish charters between 1175 and 1179. The family was also known as Mac Fheorais (see Corish ) in Ireland, named for Piers (Gaelic Feoras) de Bermyngeham, the father of Robert.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Birmingham was born in 1302, in Lincolnshire, England, his father, William de Bermingham, was 40 and his mother, Matilda Deincourt, was 34. He married Lady Elizabeth Multon about 1336. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1342, his occupation is listed as chief governor of ireland in Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Birmingham est né en 1302, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Bermingham, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Matilda Deincourt, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Multon environ 1336. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. En 1342, sa profession de son indiquée est chief governor of ireland à Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter de Birmingham 1302 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Bermingham 40 und seine Mutter Matilda Deincourt 34. Er hat um 1336 Lady Elizabeth Multon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1342 ist sein Beruf als chief governor of ireland in Irland aufgeführt.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Birmingham è nato il nel 1302 nel Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, William de Bermingham, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Deincourt, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Lady Elizabeth Multon circa 1336. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Nel 1342, la sua occupazione è indicata come chief governor of ireland nel Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Birminghamが1302年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Berminghamは40歳，母Matilda Deincourtは34歳であった。約1336年にLady Elizabeth Multonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1342年，アイルランドでの職業はchief governor of irelandと記録されている。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Birmingham(이)가 1302년 영국 링코실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Bermingham(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Deincourt(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1336년 Lady Elizabeth Multon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1342년, 그의 직업은 아일랜드에서 chief governor of ireland이었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Birmingham nasceu em 1302, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Bermingham, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Deincourt, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Multon aproximadamente 1336. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Em 1342, sua profissão aparece como chief governor of ireland em Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Birmingham родился в 1302 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Bermingham было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Deincourt, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Multon около 1336 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1342 году он работал по профессии chief governor of ireland в местности Ирландия.

sm.from 1342 to 1348: mai le 1342 e o'o I le 1348

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter de Birmingham nació en 1302, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Bermingham, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Matilda Deincourt, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Lady Elizabeth Multon aproximadamente 1336. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. En 1342, su ocupación registrada era chief governor of ireland en Irlanda.
